@moqt examples

Each example demonstrates one aspect of the MoQ client stack.

Pass ?hash=<hex> with the relay's certificate hash and optionally &url=<relay-url> (default: https://localhost:4443).

  • Simple (Playa) — Full player with UI controls using @playa/player (~30 lines of player code)
  • Connect + Catalog — WebTransport handshake, subscribe to catalog, print tracks
  • Catalog Integration — Real-server integration harness: subscribe to the namespace catalog track, display raw JSON, and materialize MSF/CMSF catalog state
  • Video Playback — Decode video with WebCodecs, render to canvas (manual wiring, 588 lines)
  • Player API — Same A/V result using @moqt/player (protocol handled by player, ~250 lines)
  • Broadcast — Publish camera or screen to a MoQ relay. Share the viewer URL for sub-second latency playback.
